info.name: Snowflake Schema API
info.description:
The Snowflake Schema API is a REST API that you can use to access, update, and perform certain actions on a Snowflake schema.

Contact Support:
 Name: Snowflake, Inc.
 Email: support@snowflake.com

query.description:
Parameter allowing support for different modes of resource creation. Possible values include:
- `errorIfExists`: Throws an error if you try to create a resource that already exists.
- `orReplace`: Automatically replaces the existing resource with the current one.
- `ifNotExists`: Creates a new resource when an alter is requested for a non-existent resource.
Type of schema to clone. Currently, Snowflake supports only `transient` and `permanent` (also represented by the empty string).
Database of the newly created schema. Defaults to the source schema's database.

variable.description: Identifier (i.e. name) for the database to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases` GET request to get a list of available databases.

request.description: Clones an existing schema, with modifiers as query parameters. You must provide the full schema definition when cloning an existing schema.

response.name: Successful request.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

response.name: Successfully accepted the request, but it is not completed yet.

query.description:
Parameter that specifies how to handle the request for a resource that does not exist:
- `true`: The endpoint does not throw an error if the resource does not exist. It returns a 200 success response, but does not take any action on the resource.
- `false`: The endpoint throws an error if the resource doesn't exist.

Whether to drop the schema if foreign keys exist that reference any tables in the schema.
- `true`: Return a warning about existing foreign key references and don't drop the schema.
- `false`: Drop the schema and all objects in the database, including tables with primary or unique keys that are referenced by foreign keys in other tables.
Default: `false`.

variable.description: Identifier (i.e. name) for the database to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases` GET request to get a list of available databases.
Identifier (i.e. name) for the resource.

request.description: Deletes the specified schema. If you enable the `ifExists` parameter, the operation succeeds even if the schema does not exist. Otherwise, a 404 failure is returned if the schema does not exist. if the drop is unsuccessful.

response.name: Successful request.

Parameter to limit the maximum number of rows returned by a command.
Parameter to enable fetching rows only following the first row whose object name matches the specified string. Case-sensitive and does not have to be the full name.
Whether to include dropped schemas that have not yet been purged. Default: `false`.

variable.description: Identifier (i.e. name) for the database to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases` GET request to get a list of available databases.

response.name: Limit Exceeded. The number of requests hit the rate limit. The application must slow down the frequency of hitting the API endpoints.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

variable.description: Identifier (i.e. name) for the database to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases` GET request to get a list of available databases.

query.description:
Parameter allowing support for different modes of resource creation. Possible values include:
- `errorIfExists`: Throws an error if you try to create a resource that already exists.
- `orReplace`: Automatically replaces the existing resource with the current one.
- `ifNotExists`: Creates a new resource when an alter is requested for a non-existent resource.
Type of schema to create. Currently, Snowflake supports only `transient` and `permanent` (also represented by the empty string).

variable.description: Identifier (i.e. name) for the database to which the resource belongs. You can use the `/api/v2/databases` GET request to get a list of available databases.

request.description: Creates a schema, with modifiers as query parameters. You must provide the full schema definition when creating a schema.

response.name: Successful request.
